mission.title: cloud-native-network: Make failure message great again
mission.description: Make failure message great again. This issue affects 1+ users.

steps.title: Identify cloud-native-network feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your cloud-native-network deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=cloud-native-network
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cloud-native-network -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Make failure message great again

steps.title: Check current resource configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant cloud-native-network resources:
```bash
kubectl get resource -A
kubectl describe resource <name> -n <namespace>
```
Right now if we had a test failed when run `cargo test`, the failure message is missing and we can not locate the assert that failed directly.  

**And if comment the tests code in `tty.rs`, the failu

steps.title: Apply the fix for Make failure message great again
steps.description:
Fix #185

This PR replaces clone(2) with double fork to create the container process. Double fork was the original implementation, and we wisely thought that we could improve it by using a single clone call. Turns out jokes on us that we can't use a single clone call. With clone, if we enter into a new pid namespace, we are OK. However, if we need to set_ns into an existing pid namespace, we are out of luck. So the right path forward needs to be double fork. For a more detailed discussion, see #

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Confirm Make failure message great again is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cloud-native-network -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: Fix #185

This PR replaces clone(2) with double fork to create the container process. Double fork was the original implementation, and we wisely thought that we could improve it by using a single clone call. Turns out jokes on us that we can't use a single clone call. With clone, if we enter into a new pid namespace, we are OK. However, if we need to set_ns into an existing pid namespace, we are o. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with cloud-native-network installed or the issue environment reproducible.
